name: node-esprima
title: node-esprima
summary: ECMAScript parsing infrastructure for multipurpose analysis
description:
ECMAScript parsing infrastructure for multipurpose analysis
Esprima is a high-performance and standard-compliant parser for ECMAScript
written in ECMAScript (Javascript).
.
This package includes the Node.js package, the source needed for
libjs-esprima and the documentation.
.
Node.js is an event-based server-side JavaScript engine.

arguments.description: Gather all line and block comments in an array
arguments.raw: --comment  Gather all line and block comments in an array

arguments.description: Include line-column location info for each syntax node
arguments.raw: --loc  Include line-column location info for each syntax node

arguments.description: Include index-based range for each syntax node
arguments.raw: --range  Include index-based range for each syntax node

arguments.description: Display the raw value of literals
arguments.raw: --raw  Display the raw value of literals

arguments.description: List all tokens in an array
arguments.raw: --tokens  List all tokens in an array

arguments.description: Tolerate errors on a best-effort basis (experimental)
arguments.raw: --tolerant  Tolerate errors on a best-effort basis (experimental)

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use esparse because ECMAScript parsing infrastructure for multipurpose analysis.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use esparse as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `esparse [options] file.js` as documented upstream for esparse.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair esparse with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `esparse --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install node-esprima. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `esparse [options] file.js`. ECMAScript parsing infrastructure for multipurpose analysis.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: ECMAScript parsing infrastructure for multipurpose analysis.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: esparse - ECMAScript Parser using Esprima.
